text: Both natural and synthetic ligands can interact with all 3 main PPAR isoforms (α, β/δ, γ) with unsaturated fatty acids and other lipid-derived molecules being the primary natural ligands the PPAR isoforms (Ferré 2004).  Numerous studies have shown the ability of synthetic ligands to bind the ligand binding domains of the PPAR isoforms (Ferré 2004; Grygiel-Górniak 2014).  This Key Event Relationship describes the binding of stressor ligands to the PPAR isoforms with either agonist or antagonist interactions which then disrupts downstream PPAR isoform nuclear signaling. The ligands that bind the PPAR isoforms either agonistically or antagonistically can disrupt proper PPAR activity and nuclear signaling for the either expression or repression of target genes.
